name: SJVN

identification.ownership_details: Navratna PSU since August 2024; Total promoter 81.85%
identification.registered_office: SJVN Corporate Office Complex, Shanan, Shimla, Himachal Pradesh

coal.notes: Zero coal operational; BUT 1,320 MW Buxar TPP under construction

hydro.notes: Nathpa Jhakri 1500 MW + Rampur 412 MW + Naitwar Mori 60 MW

solar.notes: Bikaner 1000 + Raghanesda 100 + Omkareshwar 90 + UP projects 200 + Charanka 5.6

capacity.pipeline_details: Lower Arun 669 MW (Nepal, approved Aug 2024) + Kholongchhu 600 MW (Bhutan JV)
capacity.source: SJVN Investor Presentation, NSE filings December 2025

generation.notes: FY25: ~10,647 MU total; Hydro 95%, Solar 3.6%, Wind 1.6%. FY24: 8,489 MU (Hydro 8,131 + Solar 210 + Wind 151). Q3 FY25 solar +387% YoY.
generation.source: SJVN Annual Report FY25, Quarterly Results

interest_expense.notes: INR 743 crore FY25; +54.1% YoY from 482 Cr FY24; driven by borrowing ramp-up for capex program
interest_expense.source: SJVN Annual Report FY25, CRISIL/CARE Rating Reports

total_debt.notes: INR 26,906 crore FY25; +33% YoY from 20,223 Cr FY24; D/E 1.9x up from 1.0x FY23
total_debt.source: SJVN Annual Report FY25, Balance Sheet

current_assets.notes: INR 4,466.36 crore FY25 (Cash 356 + Receivables 858 + Inventory 84 + Other 3,168); down from 4,896 Cr FY24

current_liabilities.notes: INR 4,535.76 crore FY25 (Payables 199 + Accrued 699 + ST Debt 119 + Current LT Debt 602 + Other 2,917); up from 4,103 Cr FY24

operating_cash_flow.notes: INR 2,483 crore FY25; +89.5% YoY from 1,310 Cr FY24
operating_cash_flow.source: SJVN Consolidated Cash Flow Statement FY25

capex.notes: INR ~10,000-12,000 crore annual capex program; Buxar TPP INR 13,756 Cr total

credit_rating.notes: Unanimous AA+/Stable from all three agencies

scope1.notes: Very low - primarily from vehicles/equipment; -70.3% YoY

total.notes: Total Scope 1+2 emissions - extremely low for a 3+ GW utility

coal_policy.coal_exit_details: 1,320 MW Buxar TPP under construction - first thermal project

re_targets.notes: Currently 100% RE but adding 1,320 MW coal (Buxar TPP) will reduce RE share to ~70% when commissioned

capex_allocation.clean_capex_definition: Hydro + Solar projects; Buxar TPP (coal) is sole non-clean allocation
capex_allocation.notes: Clean CapEx 63-65% by project cost (Hydro ~27% + Solar ~35%); Buxar TPP 35-38%. By capacity: 71.3% clean (3,331 MW) vs 28.7% thermal (1,320 MW).

international_expansion.notes: International hydro projects position SJVN for cross-border RE trade

coal_plants.notes: Currently 0 MW coal operational; 1,320 MW Buxar TPP under construction

esg_ratings.sustainalytics_notes: Low exposure due to hydro-dominated portfolio

debt_to_ebitda.notes: Debt 26,906 Cr / EBITDA 2,221 Cr = 12.11x - very elevated from massive 5 GW capex program

interest_coverage.notes: EBITDA 2,221 Cr / Interest 743 Cr = 2.99x - just above threshold; CRISIL reports 3.3-3.4x; down from 5.5x FY23

ffo_to_debt_pct.notes: OCF 2,483 Cr / Debt 26,906 Cr = 9.2% - high leverage relative to cash generation

current_ratio.notes: CA 4,466 Cr / CL 4,536 Cr = 0.98x - deteriorated from 1.19x FY24; liquidity tightening from capex

debt_to_capital_pct.notes: Debt 26,906 Cr / (Debt + Equity 41,085 Cr) = 65.5% - elevated from project financing; D/E 1.9x

credit_metrics_b5.notes: 1/5 metrics pass (only Interest Coverage 2.99x). B5 FAIL. All metrics now deteriorated from FY23: D/EBITDA increased from ~4x to 12.1x, Interest Coverage fell from 5.5x to 3.0x, Current Ratio from 1.19x to 0.98x. AA+ rating reflects government backing (81.85%), cost-plus tariffs with 16.5% ROE guarantee, and ₹3,000+ Cr liquidity - not standalone credit metrics.
credit_metrics_b5.source: Calculated from SJVN FY25 Audited Financials, CRISIL/CARE Ratings Aug-Nov 2025

data_gaps.notes: Scope 3 not reported - SJVN not submitted to CDP; outside BRSR Core top 250 threshold

data_resolved.source: SJVN Annual Report FY25, CRISIL/CARE Rating Reports
SJVN Consolidated Cash Flow Statement FY25

verification_checklist.coal_capacity_note: 0 MW coal operational; 1,320 MW Buxar TPP under construction (first thermal)
verification_checklist.capacity_sums_note: 1972 + 1076 + 97.6 = 3,145.6 ≈ 3,146 MW (solar split slightly different in source)
verification_checklist.credit_metrics_note: All 5/5 B5 metrics now calculated from FY25 audited financials: D/EBITDA 12.11x, ICR 2.99x, FFO/Debt 9.2%, CR 0.98x, D/Cap 65.5%
verification_checklist.b5_data_note: Complete: interest expense 743 Cr, OCF 2,483 Cr, CA 4,466 Cr, CL 4,536 Cr all sourced from audited FY25

analyst_notes: SJVN is a Navratna PSU and currently a 100% RE company (hydro 63%, solar 34%, wind 3%). Key strengths: (1) Zero operational coal - passes B1/B2/B3; (2) Negligible emissions (3,594 tCO2e total); (3) Strong government backing (81.85% promoter); (4) International expansion (Nepal, Bhutan hydro). KEY CONCERN: 1,320 MW Buxar TPP (coal) under construction will reduce RE share to ~70% when commissioned in 2025-26. This represents SJVN's transition FROM pure RE to mixed portfolio. No net-zero or SBTi commitment despite current 100% RE status. B5 CREDIT ANALYSIS (FY25 complete): Very high leverage from massive 5 GW capex program - D/EBITDA 12.11x (threshold <4.0x), Interest Coverage 2.99x (PASSES, threshold >2.5x), FFO/Debt 9.2% (threshold >20%), Current Ratio 0.98x (threshold >1.0x), Debt/Capital 65.5% (threshold <60%). Only 1/5 metrics pass. AA+ rating reflects government support (81.85%), cost-plus tariffs with 16.5% ROE guarantee, and liquidity strength - NOT standalone credit metrics. ETCB outcomes: B1 PASS (0% coal operational), B2 PASS (near-zero emissions), B3 PASS (no thermal), B4 PASS (clean CapEx 64% > 50% threshold), B5 FAIL (1/5 metrics). Expected: 4/5 Medium-high tier. Note: Once Buxar TPP operational, B1 will FAIL (coal >20% of capacity) in future assessments.
